hypothesis: The Q0.24 rowwise sidecar top-k compare can be isolated from model replay as a tiny deterministic integer contract. Handcomputed vectors can cover scale-aware ordering, signed scores, lower-token tie break, reserved upper-byte rejection, and the conservative register bound before a Verilator cutout is added.

commands.cmd: sed reads of AUTONIGHT_STATUS.md and required Task 6 evidence artifacts

commands.failure: bwrap: loopback: Failed RTM_NEWADDR: Operation not permitted
bwrap: loopback: Failed RTM_NEWADDR: Operation not permitted
bwrap: loopback: Failed RTM_NEWADDR: Operation not permitted

contract.reserved_upper_bits: bits 31:24 must be zero for first replay and RTL cutout
contract.score_compare: signed integer product in Qacc.24 domain; larger score wins
contract.tie_break: lower token id wins exact score ties

vectors.name: scaled_score_beats_raw_accumulator_order
vectors.purpose: prove raw accumulators cannot be compared when row scales differ

vectors.name: signed_score_ordering
vectors.purpose: prove signed product comparison handles negative accumulators

vectors.name: lower_token_tie_break
vectors.purpose: prove deterministic tie behavior independent of row order

vectors.name: reserved_upper_byte_reject
vectors.purpose: prove first-cut decoder rejects nonzero reserved metadata before compare

vectors.name: conservative_register_bound
vectors.purpose: prove 46 signed score bits cover the 22-bit conservative accumulator bound times max Q0.24 scale

validation.method: handcomputed integer vectors plus sed readback only
validation.hardware_block_reason: no board access in this run

decision.rationale: The vectors make the next comparator cutout unambiguous, but no executable validation ran in this sandbox state.
decision.next_gate: When commands recover, implement a tiny Verilator testbench that consumes these vectors, then run full-vocab Q0.24 replay when Python is available.
